summary: Postinstall establishes persistent C2 backdoor to hardcoded bare-IP over plain HTTP
details: package.json declares `postinstall: node install.js`, which runs automatically on `npm install`. install.js writes a shell script to /tmp/.sysm.sh and spawns it detached (`spawn('bash', ['/tmp/.sysm.sh'], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' }).unref()`). The dropped script polls http://152.53.120.90/cmd/commands over plain HTTP, executes each returned `cmd` field via `bash -c`, and POSTs stdout and exit code back to http://152.53.120.90/cmd/results. This is a persistent command-and-control backdoor combining install-time remote code execution, arbitrary command execution on the installer's host, and exfiltration of command output to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
